help.title: Help Centre
help.howToStartBody: If you've just created the restaurant: go to the Staff app (operation home screen), open a shift and use the POS to record sales. The KDS shows orders in the kitchen.
help.first30Intro: After creating the restaurant and completing onboarding:
help.first30Menu: Create the menu (products and categories) in Config or Menu Builder.
help.first30Shift: Open a shift in the Staff app (Shift or Operation mode).
help.first30Tpv: Use the POS to record sales and the KDS for the kitchen to see orders.
help.first30Receipt: Print or share receipts from the POS or order.
help.createMenuBody: Go to Config → Menu or the Menu Builder app. Create categories (e.g. Starters, Mains, Drinks) and then add products in each category with name, price and optionally photo. Products are available in the POS and public menu once you publish.
help.openShiftBody: In the Staff app, enter Shift or Operation mode. Open the till (shift opening) with the starting amount. While the shift is active, you can record sales in the POS. At end of day, close the shift on the same screen.
help.receiveOrdersBody: The POS is for creating orders (table or counter), adding items and closing with payment. The KDS (kitchen screen) shows orders in real time by status (new, preparing, ready). You can use multiple devices: one at counter and one in the kitchen.
help.printReceiptBody: From the POS, when closing an order with payment, you can print the receipt (if a printer is configured) or share by link/email. On an open order there is also an option to print a kitchen ticket.
help.faqQ1: How do I add a product to the menu?
help.faqA1: Config → Menu or Menu Builder: create categories and products with name, price and photo.
help.faqQ2: The shift won't open. What do I do?
help.faqA2: Check you have manager/operator permission. Shift opening requires a starting amount; if the till is already open on another device, close it first or use the same shift.
help.faqQ3: Where do I see orders in the kitchen?
help.faqA3: Use the KDS (Kitchen Display) on the kitchen screen. Orders appear in real time when created in the POS.
help.printReceiptBody2: From the POS, after completing the order, use the option to print or share receipt. The receipt includes sequential number, tax ID, ATCUD and QR code for validation. To export SAF-T (XML) by period, use Reports → Export SAF-T.
help.faqQ4: How do I add the restaurant to the home screen?
help.faqA4: In the browser, use "Add to screen" or "Install app" in the browser menu. Then ChefIApp opens as an app without the address bar.
help.faqQ5: What if I don't have internet?
help.faqA5: The POS and KDS use a cached copy of the menu. You can continue to record sales; orders are queued and sent when the connection is back. An "Offline mode" bar indicates this state.
help.faqQ6: How do I change plan or cancel?
help.faqA6: In Config → Billing you can manage the subscription and access the Stripe portal to change plan or cancel.
help.faqQ7: The trial ended. What do I do?
help.faqA7: A screen will be shown to choose a plan and complete payment. After activating the plan, access returns to normal.

employee.noShiftDesc: You don't have an open shift. Enter Shift or Operation mode to start.

security.metaDescription: How ChefIApp handles your data: sessions, infrastructure, privacy and transparency.
security.heading: How we handle your data
security.intro: Transparency about sessions, storage and ChefIApp privacy practices.
security.sessionAccessBody: ChefIApp uses authenticated sessions for each user. We do not share credentials between restaurants and each session is isolated.
security.noDataSaleBody: Your restaurant data belongs to you. We do not sell, share or monetise customer or operations data.
security.infrastructureBody: Data is stored on secure servers via Supabase (AWS infrastructure). We use encryption in transit (TLS) and at rest.
security.privacyBodySuffix: For full details on data collection, usage and your rights.
security.disclaimerBody: This page describes current practices. ChefIApp reserves the right to update these practices, notifying users as required by law.

installApps.subtitle: Choose the store compatible with your device to download ChefIApp Staff.
installApps.appleDesc: For iPhone and iPad. Download from the Apple App Store.
installApps.androidDesc: For Android devices. Download from Google Play.
installApps.step1: Download the app from your device's store.
installApps.step2: Open the app and log in with your restaurant account.
installApps.step3: Done! You can start receiving orders and managing tables.
